title: Austroblechnum microphyllum
taxonomy: Austroblechnum microphyllum is a species of fern in the family Blechnaceae. It belongs to the genus Austroblechnum. Within the Blechnaceae family, Austroblechnum microphyllum is classified as a terrestrial fern. More specifically, it is a member of the order Polypodiales. Austroblechnum microphyllum is native to Australia and New Zealand.

intro: Austroblechnum microphyllum, also known as the Tasmanian tree fern, is a species of fern native to Australia. The plant has been traditionally used in herbal medicine, and its health benefits are attributed to its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which can aid in the treatment of various health conditions, including wounds and infections. The bioactive constituents of Austroblechnum microphyllum include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and terpenoids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties.
